labeler: project author (truth known from the permit generator)
notes: ILLUSTRATIVE gold set for the bundled SYNTHETIC permit. The true obligations are known because the project authored the PDF (sample_data/make_sample_permit.py). This exists ONLY to exercise and self-test the evaluation harness end to end. It is NOT the expert answer key required by the paper (Part A5.4), which must be built by a domain expert on REAL permits with documented labelers and adjudication. Do not report these numbers as the paper's measured results.

obligations.description: Nitrogen oxides (NOx) emissions from Boiler Unit B-1 shall not exceed 30 ppm, corrected to 15 percent oxygen, on a 30-day rolling average.
Sulfur dioxide (SO2) emissions from Boiler Unit B-1 shall not exceed 0.30 lb/MMBtu on a 30-day rolling average.
Carbon monoxide (CO) emissions shall not exceed 100 ppm at the stack outlet.
Particulate matter (PM10) emissions shall not exceed 0.030 gr/dscf.
Volatile organic compounds (VOC) emissions from the coating line shall not exceed 25 tons/yr on a 12-month rolling total.
Visible emissions from any stack shall not exceed 20 percent opacity.
The pH of the discharge from Outfall 001 shall be maintained between 6.0 and 9.0 standard units.
Five-day biochemical oxygen demand (BOD5) shall not exceed 30 mg/L as a monthly average.
Total suspended solids (TSS) shall not exceed 30 mg/L as a monthly average.
The temperature of the discharge shall not exceed 32 deg C.
Fecal coliform bacteria shall not exceed 200 cfu/100 mL as a monthly geometric mean.
The discharge flow from Outfall 001 shall not exceed 5.0 MGD.
The permittee shall submit Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMRs) within 28 days after the end of each monitoring period.
The permittee shall maintain all monitoring records, including calibration and maintenance logs, for a period of five years and make them available to the Administrator upon request.
The permittee shall report any exceedance of an emission or effluent limit by telephone within 24 hours and in writing within 5 days of becoming aware of the exceedance.
